description: Safe-default configuration for production GraphQL servers, applicable across Apollo Server, graphql-yoga, graphene, gqlgen, Hot Chocolate, etc.

anti_patterns.rule: Introspection enabled in production trivializes recon; disable in production builds
Missing max_query_depth allows exponential nested resource consumption
Missing max_query_complexity allows fan-out DoS via wide selection sets
Unlimited aliases enable N-fold amplification of a single resolver call
Unbounded operation batches enable login / password-reset / rate-limit bypass via N operations per request
Endpoint-level auth without per-field auth leaks sensitive fields through a single permitted query
Apollo default errors include schema suggestions (didYouMean), which leak schema in production
JSON / Scalar typed inputs bypass schema validation; validate JSON payloads explicitly
graphql-upload without size and MIME validation has historical CVEs and recurring DoS patterns
CDN cache keyed on URL serves cross-tenant data because GraphQL POSTs share the URL
